description: RGB shows a coastal shoreline with partial cloud cover and greenish-teal near-shore water with no obvious surface film or sheen; SWIR confirms water as dark/black and vegetation as bright green with no anomalous signatures in the water.
labeler_notes: Cloud patches reduce coverage of the offshore zone but enough coast is visible to assess; no oil slick signature detected.

description: RGB is dominated by thick cloud cover with only a small sliver of coastline visible in the lower portion; SWIR is similarly saturated and cloud-dominated, providing no usable view of the water surface.
labeler_notes: Ground obscured by heavy cloud in both bands; insufficient surface data to assess hazard.

description: RGB shows forested coast and settlement with significant cloud interruptions; SWIR shows vegetation as bright green and water as dark with no anomalous SWIR signal over the coastal water.
labeler_notes: Cloud cover limits full coverage of offshore zone; visible water areas show no oil signature in either band.

description: RGB is nearly entirely white, dominated by thick cloud with only trace fragments of land visible at the edges; SWIR is similarly saturated with cyan/white tones indicating extensive cloud, providing no usable ground data.
labeler_notes: Both bands effectively fully cloud-obscured; cannot assess surface conditions.

description: RGB shows a coastal industrial zone with scattered cloud and a distinctly dark elongated patch extending into the coastal water; SWIR shows an anomalous red/pink area over the coastal zone where RGB shows the dark water patch, consistent with a hydrocarbon or industrial surface film.
labeler_notes: Cloud patches reduce full coverage but the coastal anomaly is visible in a partially clear window; SWIR red signature over water warrants HIGH classification.

description: Both RGB and SWIR are essentially blank white with no visible ground features; the frame is completely cloud-covered or contains no-data fill.
labeler_notes: No usable imagery in either band; entirely obscured.

description: RGB is nearly all white with only a tiny fragment of ground visible at the bottom-left corner; SWIR is similarly blank and near-featureless, indicating near-total cloud or no-data coverage.
labeler_notes: Effectively no usable ground information in either band.

description: RGB shows arid inland terrain with a road corridor, sparse agricultural patches, and partial cloud at the top; SWIR shows predominantly pink/reddish tones indicating dry bare soil across the scene with no fire, flooding, or oil-related anomaly.
labeler_notes: Inland arid scene away from the coast; no hazard evidence in either band.

description: RGB shows a clear, cloud-free view of the Peruvian coastal city and adjacent dark ocean water with a sharp shoreline contrast; SWIR shows the ocean uniformly dark and the urban/bare terrain as pink/magenta with no anomalous red or film-like signature over the water.
labeler_notes: Good quality image of the spill area taken after the event peak, but neither band shows a confirmable surface oil signature; downlinkable for monitoring context.

description: RGB shows dry inland urban and suburban terrain with roads, sparse green patches, and extensive bare sandy ground; SWIR shows yellow/cream and pink-red tones consistent with dry soil and minimal vegetation, with no hazard indicators.
labeler_notes: Inland scene well away from the coast and spill zone; no hazard evidence.
